channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇯🇵🛢️ Japan secures alternative oil supplies to replace Middle East crude for summer
item.description: Japanese oil refiners will be able to secure sufficient substitute crude and petroleum products to replace Middle East supplies for summer, as the Iran conflict disrupts traditional supply chains.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇧🇾 US asks Ukraine to ease Belarus potash restrictions amid food security concerns
item.description: The United States is urging Ukraine to relax restrictions on potash fertilizer imports from Belarus and press European allies to follow suit, Bloomberg News reported.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russian attacks kill two in Ukraine as Kyiv drones strike central Russia
item.description: Two people were killed and 19 injured in Russian attacks on Ukraine overnight, while Ukraine sent drones to strike industrial areas in central Russia including the Stavropol region.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President says China undermining peace, warns against annexation
item.description: President Lai Ching-te stated Taiwan would never accept annexation by China, saying the island's future cannot be decided by external forces. The comments drew an angry reaction from Beijing.

item.title: 🛢️ Chinese supertankers exit Hormuz as Trump and Vance signal Iran deal progress
item.description: Two Chinese supertankers carrying 4 million barrels of crude exited the Strait of Hormuz after waiting two months, as US officials expressed optimism about negotiations with Tehran.

item.title: ⚔️ Russia displays nuclear warhead transfers in major military exercise
item.description: Russia released footage of troops delivering nuclear warheads to mobile Iskander-M launch systems and moving them undetected to launch sites as part of a major military exercise.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Four US LNG vessels sailing to China following Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Four liquefied natural gas vessels are en route to China from the US, marking the first direct LNG cargoes during Trump's second term amid ongoing trade negotiations.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon kill 19, including children and women
item.description: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 19 people, including four women and three children, amid ongoing Israel-Hezbollah conflict despite U.S.-brokered ceasefire extensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
item.description: The US Senate moved forward with a procedural vote to curb presidential authority on Iran military actions, though the resolution faces significant hurdles before becoming law.

item.title: ☢️🇷🇺 Russia Conducts Major Nuclear Drills Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: Russia began three days of nuclear drills including ballistic and cruise missile launches, the defense ministry announced, as Moscow continues its confrontation with the West over Ukraine.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 NATO Commander: US Troop Cuts from Europe to Take Years
item.description: NATO's top commander said US troop withdrawals from Europe will stretch over years to give allies time to develop replacement capabilities, maintaining alliance defense posture.

item.title: ⚠️🇱🇻 Russia Warns Latvia Against Ukraine Drone Launches
item.description: Russia's UN ambassador claimed Ukraine plans to launch military drones from Latvia and other Baltic states, warning that NATO membership would not protect them from retaliation.

item.title: 💰🌐 G7 Finance Ministers Address Trade Imbalances, China Concerns
item.description: G7 finance ministers agreed on the need to tackle trade imbalances in a fragmented global economy, with US Treasury Secretary Bessent calling for protections against cheap Chinese imports.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Xi Hosts Putin in Beijing, Hails Ties as "Calm Amid Chaos"
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Vladimir Putin for a state visit just days after hosting Trump, calling Russia-China relations a force of stability. Xi urged an "early end" to the Iran war and criticized "unilateral hegemonic currents" in a veiled jab at the US.

item.title: 🇺🇦 UN: Over 15,800 Killed in Russia-Ukraine War Since 2022
item.description: The UN reports at least 15,850 people including 791 children have been killed since Russia's full-scale invasion. The actual figures are likely significantly higher as peace talks remain stalled.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 EU Strikes Deal on US Trade Pact Ahead of Trump Tariff Deadline
item.description: The European Parliament and Council reached a late-night provisional agreement to remove import duties on US goods, keeping the bloc on track to meet the July 4 deadline and avoid higher US tariffs on European goods.

item.title: 🇬🇧⛽ UK quietly sanctions Russian oil via waivers as energy costs surge
item.description: Britain issued licenses allowing imports of diesel and jet fuel made from Russian crude processed in third countries, and maritime transport of Russian LNG. The move follows similar US waivers as fuel prices spike during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery and chemical plant for second time in a week
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit the Lukoil-Nizhegorodnefteorgsintez refinery in Nizhny Novgorod Oblast and the Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant in Stavropol Krai overnight. Both facilities had been struck earlier in the week.

item.title: 🇨🇿🇪🇺 Czechia proposes restrictions on Ukrainian refugee benefits
item.description: The Czech Interior Ministry proposed legal changes requiring refugees not to leave Schengen for more than 30 days, be economically active to receive benefits, and register vehicles. EU discussions on long-term protections continue amid uncertainty.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Vance says US not planning to send Iranian uranium to Russia
item.description: VP Vance dismissed reports that the US would allow Russia to take possession of Iran enriched uranium as part of a peace deal, saying it was not the current plan. Tehran reportedly offered to transfer 400kg of enriched uranium to Russia in leaked settlement details.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇨🇳 Putin declares Russia-China ties at unprecedented level, invites Xi to Moscow
item.description: President Putin told Xi Jinping that bilateral relations have reached an unprecedented level during Beijing talks, extending an invitation for Xi to visit Russia next year.

item.title: 🇷🇺💰 Russia considers joint projects with US and China, says sovereign fund chief
item.description: Kirill Dmitriev, head of Russia sovereign wealth fund RDIF, indicated Moscow is exploring potential joint economic initiatives with both Washington and Beijing.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏦 Fed Chair nominee Warsh signals shift on independence, unsettling global central bankers
item.description: Kevin Warsh suggestion that Fed independence may not fully extend to crisis-fighting abroad has raised concerns among central banking peers worldwide.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Treasury Secretary Bessent calls for stronger action against Iran financing networks
item.description: Scott Bessent urged allies to more forcefully disrupt Iran financial channels and announced a review to scrub outdated sanctions designations for better enforcement.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US not rushing to extend China tariff truce, Bessent says
item.description: Treasury Secretary indicated the administration has time to negotiate extensions before the November expiration, signaling no urgency on the critical minerals deal.
